subject: Geography
title: Erosion: Mountains in Reverse
description: A quick win: understand Erosion: Mountains in Reverse.

hook.text: The moment a mountain rises, wind, rain, and ice start tearing it back down.

buildup.text: Glaciers carve U-shaped valleys. Rivers cut V-shaped gorges. Frost shatters rock.

discovery.text: The Matterhorn's sharp pyramid shape was carved by glaciers grinding from all sides.

twist.text: Without erosion, the Himalayas would be twice their current height.

climax.text: Sediment from eroding mountains builds new land far downstream in river deltas.

punchline.text: Mountains don't last. They just move downhill, slowly.

quiz.question: What carved the Matterhorn's distinctive pyramid shape?
